insight.headline: Storefront API update
insight.what_changed: The Storefront API now supports the @inContext directive with an optional channelId argument, allowing developers to apply a specific sales channel's context to an entire query. This change is available in Storefront API version 2026-10. The @inContext directive can be used to fetch data specific to a particular sales channel.
insight.developer_impact: Developers using the Storefront API should update their queries to include the channelId argument when necessary. This change provides more flexibility in querying data and can help improve the performance of storefronts. Developers should review their existing queries and update them to take advantage of this new feature. The GraphQL Admin API and Storefront API should be used in conjunction to manage sales channels and their associated data.
insight.merchant_impact: Merchants will benefit from more accurate and sales-channel-specific data being displayed on their storefronts, leading to a better customer experience. This change can also help merchants manage their sales channels more effectively.
insight.why_it_matters: This update matters because it provides more flexibility and control over sales channel data, allowing developers to build more customized and effective storefronts. It also aligns with Shopify's goal of providing a more unified and streamlined commerce experience.

also_worth_knowing.text: Card deposit endpoint now requires mTLS certificate

updates.title: Physical inventory feature preview
updates.summary: The physical inventory feature preview is available in the unstable version of the GraphQL Admin API, allowing developers to build and test against new inventory APIs.
The Shopify CLI has been updated to version 4.5.2, which includes fixes for theme commands and updates to the theme-tools packages.

updates.title: Card deposit endpoint now requires mTLS certificate
updates.summary: The card deposit endpoint now requires a Shopify-issued mTLS client certificate, affecting apps that store cardholder data.

updates.title: @shopify/cli-hydrogen@13.0.2
updates.summary: The @shopify/cli-hydrogen package has been updated to version 13.0.2, adding non-interactive shop and storefront creation flags and a force flag to bypass confirmation prompts.

updates.title: Updated App Store requirements: 4.1.2 Use a unique name for your app
updates.summary: The App Store requirements have been updated to include a unique name for each app, reducing merchant confusion over identical and similar app names.

updates.title: Storefront API `@inContext` supports `channelId`
updates.summary: The Storefront API now supports the channelId argument in the @inContext directive, allowing developers to apply a specific sales channel's context to an entire query.

updates.title: `productId`, `title`,`variantSku`, and `variantTitle` fields added to `ExchangeLineItem`
updates.summary: The ExchangeLineItem type now includes productId, title, variantSku, and variantTitle fields, providing more detailed information about exchange line items.

updates.title: POS UI Extensions 2026-07 adds discount allocations to bundle components
updates.summary: The POS UI Extensions API now includes discount allocation details for product bundle components in cart line item data, allowing apps to access component-level discount allocations.
The Shopify CLI has been updated to version 4.5.1, which includes a bump to cli-hydrogen version 13.0.1.
The Shopify CLI has been updated to version 4.5.0, introducing new store commands, including listing and store creation.

updates.title: @shopify/cli-hydrogen@13.0.1
updates.summary: The @shopify/cli-hydrogen package has been updated to version 13.0.1, which includes improvements to command metadata and build error handling.

updates.title: @shopify/create-hydrogen@5.0.38
updates.summary: The @shopify/create-hydrogen package has been updated to version 5.0.38, which includes an explicit app alias and improvements to JavaScript project configuration.

recap.headline: Shopify updates: new inventory APIs, mTLS certificate requirements, and Hydrogen improvements

themes.title: New Features and APIs
themes.detail: The physical inventory feature preview is available in the unstable version of the GraphQL Admin API, and the Storefront API `@inContext` directive now supports `channelId`. Additionally, new fields have been added to the `ExchangeLineItem` type, and POS UI Extensions now include discount allocations to bundle components.

themes.title: Security and Authentication
themes.detail: The card deposit endpoint now requires a Shopify-issued mTLS client certificate, and apps that store cardholder data must be updated to use this certificate. The `@shopify/cli-hydrogen` package has also been updated to preserve actionable build errors during deployment.

themes.title: Tooling and Development
themes.detail: The Shopify CLI has been updated to version 4.5.2, with improvements to theme commands and the addition of new store commands. The `@shopify/cli-hydrogen` package has also been updated to version 13.0.2, with new flags for non-interactive shop and storefront creation.

actions.text: Enable the physical inventory feature preview on a development store
Update apps to use the new mTLS client certificate for the card deposit endpoint
Test and deploy updates to use the new `@inContext` directive with `channelId`
